text: This work presents the state-of-the-art in overlapping community detection methodologies, evaluation indices and test data sets. We compare fifteen algorithms. In addition to indices relevant to community detection (Normalized Mutual Information, Omega index, etc.), we also propose a set of indices to characterize the capability of overlapping community detection algorithms in detecting overlapping nodes and analyze the problems of overdetection and underdetection in different cases. Experiments show that the algorithms of SLPA, OSLOM, Game and COPRA can detect communities with higher quality in the case of low overlapping density graphs. Also, the algorithms of SLPA and Game have more stable performance in the case of graphs with high overlapping density and high overlapping diversity. At the same time, the detection of communities in the high overlapping density and diversity graphs is also a difficult task. Most real-world networks have a small number of overlapping nodes (less than 30% on average) and these nodes are usually contained in 2 or 3 communities.
